identity.name: The Princess Who Kept the Tower
identity.story: Let her hair down only for the visitors she chose, cut it the day the choosing got tiresome, and kept the tower anyway — turned out the view was the part she'd have missed, not the rescue. Traded the height for the tallest tree in town when she moved down; the argument in her old story was never about how far up, only who got to decide. Her hair still grows a foot overnight if she forgets to plait it, which she considers proof enough it was never really about the tower.

relationships.kind: the other retired doorway-keeper in town; they compare notes on visitors who used to arrive by unusual means and agree neither method deserves a comeback
trades a windowbox of tower greens for whatever's honest at the garden stall, and won't say which of them started the custom

behavior.label: the Princess who kept the tower's view

children.label: walking down to the heart of things
leaving a windowbox of tower greens on the garden hedge for the Kinder Sister to find
walking over to Widow Hood's step
agreeing, again, that neither of their old methods deserves a comeback
walking back to the tall tree
climbing to the top for the view, same as any tower ever gave her
